title: arctostaphylos tomentosa subsp. tomentosiformis
intro:
Arctostaphylos tomentosa subsp. tomentosiformis, also known as Island Woolly Monkey Flower or Island Woolly Manzanita, is a subspecies of the Arctostaphylos tomentosa plant, a type of evergreen shrub native to the Channel Islands of California.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Arctostaphylos tomentosa subsp. tomentosiformis include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications such as salves and creams to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
